last_item: Query — surface the server's executed_sql (possibly auto-LIMIT-rewritten) in a compact block so users see what actually ran (med/S).
last_result: REJECTED on restraint (full review panel). Implemented the restraint-aware way: a compact 'Executed SQL' <pre> block (mirroring Settings.tsx) rendered ONLY when normalize(executed_sql) !== normalize(submitted) — i.e. the server actually rewrote the query; verbatim runs add zero surface. Captured submitted SQL into state, fixed the test fixture to a realistic executed_sql, added hidden-on-verbatim + shown-on-rewrite assertions; 6 Query tests green. Panel: 3 SHIP (UX heuristics — visibility of system status; Sam — trust win, vague→concrete; Priya — exact LIMIT value, zero friction) but the RESTRAINT CRITIC BLOCKED: redundant decoration — the ONLY rewrite path is the auto-LIMIT injection, which always co-occurs with the existing 'Results limited for safety' badge + 'Add your own LIMIT…' copy that already explain a LIMIT was applied; the block just restates it. VERIFIED the critic's load-bearing assumption in internal/pg/guard/guard.go: Check() mutates SQL solely via injectLimit, which sets cls.HasLimit→limited=true — no rewrite path changes the SQL without flipping limited. So the assumption holds and REJECT stands; the restraint blocker is never overruled by the three ships. Reverted both files clean; recorded the lesson ('honest-state surfacing only wins when the data is otherwise hidden — here the limited copy already conveys it; check existing UI before adding an honest-state surface') in learnings.md; marked backlog item rejected. No code shipped. Next top item: Alerts — add Tooltip definitions to the bare 'Sustained' / 'Cooldown' column headers (med/S).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn — rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres — minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
